findings.evidence: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Finsupp/LSum.lean:143 `noncomputable def lift : (X → M) ≃+ ((X →₀ R) →ₗ[R] M)`; addMonoidHomLequivInt in Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Equiv/Basic.lean; FreeAbelianGroup.liftAddEquiv and equivFinsupp in Mathlib/Algebra/FreeAbelianGroup/Finsupp.lean. The file itself builds the def from Finsupp.liftAddHom + zmultiplesHom (lines 61-73).
findings.fix: Derive freeAbelianCharEquiv by transporting Finsupp.lift (R := ℤ) (or FreeAbelianGroup.liftAddEquiv ∘ FreeAbelianGroup.equivFinsupp) through addMonoidHomLequivInt and the AddMonoidHom.toMultiplicativeLeft / MonoidHom.toAdditiveRight tags, eliminating the hand-written liftAddHom/zmultiplesHom inverse proofs; or add a comment justifying the fresh construction.
findings.issue: freeAbelianCharEquiv : (Multiplicative (σ →₀ ℤ) →* M) ≃* (σ → M) re-derives, via liftAddHom + zmultiplesHom and bespoke left_inv/right_inv proofs, content already packaged as Mathlib's Finsupp.lift (R=ℤ case): Finsupp.lift : (X → M) ≃+ ((X →₀ R) →ₗ[R] M). Over ℤ this gives (σ → M) ≃+ ((σ →₀ ℤ) →+ M) via addMonoidHomLequivInt, which is exactly this equivalence's additive shadow (Additive/Multiplicative-tagged).

summary: SplitTorus.lean reuses existing TauCeti/Mathlib API cleanly. The new freeAbelianCharEquiv has no verbatim duplicate (no multiplicative free-abelian-character equivalence exists in Mathlib), but it is the R=ℤ case of Mathlib's Finsupp.lift up to type tags and is re-derived from scratch.
